title: ECP Baton v1 (v2-architecture)
description: Output of the Layer-0 acquirer subagent for one device. Captures DOM structure, screenshots, interactable element index with stable e<int> identifiers + absolute pixel coordinates, deterministic section→cluster routing, capture state, and viewport metadata. Specialists in Layer 1 reference baton.elements[].e_index in their finding emissions; Layer-4 render resolves hotspots via dictionary lookup against baton.elements. Authored 2026-04-27 deepen-plan pass; renamed from baton-v2.json to baton-v1.json for naming-convention consistency with finding-v1.json and cluster-emission-v1.json.

schema_version.description: Version of the baton schema. Bump on backward-incompatible changes.

engagement_id.description: The audit engagement this baton belongs to. Format: YYYY-MM-DD-<8 hex>.

device.description: Which device viewport this baton captured. The acquirer dispatches one subagent per device.

url.description: Source URL captured. Empty string when source_mode='file'.

viewport.description: Viewport metadata at capture time. Specialists making position claims (above-fold, below-fold, sticky) require viewport.height to compute fold position deterministically. dpr_actual vs dpr_requested catches the §3.10 / §18.3.4 chromium_headless_shell DPR fallback class.

dpr_requested.description: Device pixel ratio requested by the acquirer (e.g., 3 for iPhone 14 emulation).

dpr_actual.description: Device pixel ratio actually achieved during capture. When dpr_actual != dpr_requested, the chromium_headless_shell silently downgraded; specialists making contrast/text-size claims should be aware.

capture_state.description: Hydration and overlay state at capture time. Surfaces conditions that affect downstream specialist reasoning: a non-hydrated SPA produces a baton that mis-routes specialists; an undismissed cookie banner occludes elements visually but they exist in DOM at scrollY=0.

hydration.description: post-hydration: networkidle reached + DOMContentLoaded + reasonable settle time. partial-hydration: page reached interactive but residual JS still mutating DOM. pre-hydration: capture happened before SPA mounted.

overlays_detected.description: Modals, banners, and overlays the acquirer detected. Acquirer dismisses them before final capture where possible; this list records what was dismissed (or attempted).

e_index.description: The baton element index of the overlay container.

dismissed.description: Whether the acquirer successfully dismissed the overlay before final capture.

page_height_px.description: Total scrollable page height at capture time, in pixels. Source-of-truth for above-fold/below-fold computations. Source: documentElement.scrollHeight.

elements.description: Every captured interactable element on the page, indexed e0..eN in DOM order. Specialists reference these by e_index in finding ELEMENT field; renderer hotspots resolve via dictionary lookup. Mobile device-aware additions (closes §24.7 #5): drawer-nav, tab-bar, and sticky elements are always-included regardless of viewport state, so off-canvas mobile UI still has stable e<int> coverage.

e_index.description: Stable index identifier for this element. Format 'e<int>' where int is the 0-based position in the elements array. Stable across consumers within one engagement.

tag.description: Lowercase tag name (button, a, input, form, img, etc.).

selector.description: Best-effort CSS selector for the element. Non-authoritative — specialists must reference by e_index, not by selector. Used by render layer fallback only.

rect.description: Absolute pixel coordinates of the element's bounding rectangle, captured at scroll_y_at_capture. Renderer uses these directly; no fuzzy matching.

scroll_y_at_capture.description: The scrollY position when this element was captured. Renderer pairs this with section screenshots to place hotspots on the right screenshot panel.

role.description: ARIA role or implicit role (button, link, heading, image, navigation, banner, etc.).

accessible_name.description: Computed accessible name from accname composition (visible text, aria-label, aria-labelledby, alt, title). Specialists use this to find elements by content (e.g., 'Add to Cart').

text_content.description: Visible text content of the element (truncated). May differ from accessible_name when icons or hidden text contribute.

is_above_fold.description: True if the element's top edge is within viewport.height of scroll_y=0 at capture. Specialists making fold-position claims should cite this rather than infer.

is_sticky.description: True if the element has CSS position: sticky or fixed at capture time. Sticky elements appear at multiple scroll positions; renderer surfaces them on every section screenshot.

is_offscreen.description: True if the element exists in DOM but has display:none, visibility:hidden, or aria-hidden=true. Mobile drawers and collapsed accordions surface here even though they're not in current viewport.

sections.description: Page sections identified by the acquirer's section-detection logic. Each section has a label and a cluster array indicating which specialist clusters should audit content within this section. Section→cluster routing is pinned to deterministic Python keyword rules (Phase A.4 of v2 plan); not LLM-emitted per-run, closes the §22.1 #2 / §23.2 #2 / §24.2 #3 cross-device routing inconsistency. SECTIONS MUST BE DISJOINT BY scroll_y RANGE (Phase M, 2026-05-01): sections[i].scroll_y_bottom < sections[i+1].scroll_y_top for all adjacent pairs. The acquirer normalizes overlapping scroll ranges before writing the baton — see workflows/acquire.md Step 2 'disjoint sections' rule. Overlapping sections silently break section-bottom-overlay placement: the bottom of section N physically falls inside section N+1's screenshot. The renderer also defends against malformed batons via _effective_section_bottom() in scripts/report/v2_markers.py, but the baton itself should not require the defense.

label.description: Human-readable section label (e.g., 'Hero banner and navigation header'). Used in agent-behavior-log and audit-trace.log.

slug.description: Slug-style identifier matching the surface field on findings (e.g., 'hero', 'product-cards', 'primary-cta', 'footer-policy-links'). Stable across runs of the same template.

clusters.description: Which specialist clusters should audit content within this section. Pinned to deterministic Python keyword rules; LLM is not authoritative on cluster assignment per-run. Empty array means the section is global (header/footer included in every cluster's context).

scroll_y_top.description: Pixel position of section's top edge.

scroll_y_bottom.description: Pixel position of section's bottom edge. MUST be strictly less than the next section's scroll_y_top (Phase M disjoint-sections invariant). The acquirer's section-detection step normalizes raw capture geometry to disjoint logical regions before writing — see workflows/acquire.md Step 2.

overlap_reason.description: Optional escape hatch: when set, this section is allowed to overlap with the previous one for a documented reason (e.g., a sticky element span captured intentionally across two sections). Most overlaps are capture artifacts and the acquirer should normalize them away rather than set this field. Omit when sections are disjoint.

screenshot_ref.description: Filename of the section screenshot (e.g., 'section-3.jpg' or 'section-3-mobile.jpg'). Path is relative to engagement directory. Renderer resolves hotspots via this reference.

page_head.description: Structured extraction of <head> content. Specialists in content-seo and trust-credibility cite from this; other clusters typically ignore.

schema_jsonld.description: Array of parsed JSON-LD structured data blocks. Type/properties per schema.org.

telemetry.description: Optional acquirer self-reported runtime metrics. Used by Phase 2 parity testing and cost canaries.

chromium_binary.description: Identifier for the chromium variant used (chromium-1217, chromium_headless_shell-1217, etc.). Tracks the §3.10 / §18.3.4 environment dependency.
